MedBeacon · Sales Intelligence

SEP-1 Prospect Finder

Hospitals ranked by CMS SEP-1 sepsis-bundle compliance. Lowest scores first — the widest care gap is the strongest reason to buy. Phase columns show where the bundle breaks (severe-sepsis vs septic-shock, 3hr vs 6hr).

Reporting period: Jul 1, 2024 – Jun 30, 2025 · CMS data refresh Q2 2026
EHR qualifier — live: EHR vendor joined in from the ONC CHPL hospital–Promoting Interoperability linkage (latest attestation year). Use the EHR vendor filter to isolate Oracle Health (Cerner) — your Red Rover integration target. Coverage ~89% of reporting hospitals; the rest show as Unknown (typically psychiatric, children's, or non–PI attesters).
Source. CMS Provider Data Catalog — Timely & Effective Care (yv7e-xc69) joined to Hospital General Information (xubh-q36u) on Facility ID.
Period. Jul 1, 2024 – Jun 30, 2025. Snapshot downloaded May 2026; refresh by re-exporting the CSVs (CMS updates quarterly).
SEP-1. Appropriate care for severe sepsis and septic shock. Higher is better; ranked lowest first.
Phase columns. SS-3 / SS-6 = severe sepsis 3hr / 6hr. Shk-3 / Shk-6 = septic shock 3hr / 6hr.
Missing score. Usually low case volume or non-reporting, not poor care — toggle “Reporting only” off to inspect.
Mandate flag. NY, NJ, IL, OH, WI (approximate). Verify current state mandate status before outreach.
EHR vendor. Derived from the ONC CHPL hospital–Promoting Interoperability linkage (hospital-promoting-interoperability-chpl-linkage.csv, healthit.gov). Per facility we take the latest attestation year and pick the inpatient EHR with the most listed certified products, ignoring portals, public-health reporting modules, and direct-messaging utilities. Approximate — verify before outreach.
